description: Object-oriented design problems: capacity-bounded structures, registration/release, pluggable selection and eviction policies, time-windowed counters, rate limiters, frequency tracking, and reservoir sampling. Practice for live-coding rounds (Revolut Load Balancer, URL Shortener, Money Transfer, Rate Limiter).

problems.name: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
problems.relevance: Closest analogue to Revolut Load Balancer — register/deregister/random-select with O(1) guarantees.
problems.description: Implement RandomizedSet with insert(val), remove(val), getRandom() all in O(1) average. getRandom must return each element with equal probability.

examples.input: insert(1), remove(2), insert(2), getRandom(), remove(1), insert(2), getRandom()
examples.output: true, false, true, 1 or 2, true, false, 2
examples.explanation: getRandom should return 1 or 2 randomly. After removing 1, only 2 remains.

constraints: There will be at least one element when getRandom is called
hints: Hash map alone gives O(1) insert/remove but not O(1) random
Array alone gives O(1) random but O(n) remove
Combine: array for storage + hash map of value → index. To remove, swap with last element and pop.
follow_ups: Add a max_size and raise CapacityExceededError on insert
Swap getRandom for round-robin (cursor + modulo)
Add weighted random (cumulative-sum + bisect)
Make it thread-safe with a single Lock

problems.name: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1) - Duplicates Allowed
problems.relevance: Variant for weighted/duplicate servers in a load balancer.
problems.description: Same as 380 but values can repeat. getRandom must return each element with probability proportional to its count.
hints: Replace dict[int, int] with dict[int, set[int]] of indices
On remove, pick any index from the set, swap with last, update both maps
follow_ups: Use as backing store for a WeightedRandomBalancer where each server registers N times by weight

problems.name: Design Phone Directory
problems.relevance: get/check/release with a fixed pool size — same shape as register/deregister with max_size.
problems.description: Design a phone directory with maxNumbers slots: get() returns any free number, check(n) is true if free, release(n) frees a number.
hints: Maintain a set of available numbers and a queue/deque for fast get
On release, push back if currently used
follow_ups: Replace integer pool with arbitrary server addresses
Make thread-safe with a Lock

problems.name: Seat Reservation Manager
problems.relevance: reserve/unreserve smallest available — capacity-bounded resource pool, same shape as least-loaded server selection.
problems.description: Manage n seats numbered 1..n. reserve() returns the smallest-numbered free seat, unreserve(seatNumber) frees it.
hints: Min-heap of free seat numbers gives O(log n) for both ops
Lazy initialization: start with [1] and a counter, push 2..n on demand
follow_ups: Adapt to a LeastConnectionsBalancer — heap keyed by (active_conns, server_id)
Handle stale heap entries when a server is deregistered

problems.name: LRU Cache
problems.relevance: Capacity-bounded structure with eviction policy — directly relevant to URL Shortener's MAX_URLS cap.
problems.description: Design get(key) and put(key, value) both O(1). Capacity is fixed; on overflow evict the least-recently-used entry.
hints: OrderedDict gets you O(1) move-to-end and popitem(last=False)
From scratch: dict + doubly-linked list, head=most-recent, tail=LRU
follow_ups: Implement without OrderedDict — dict + doubly-linked list
Add TTL per entry
Make thread-safe with a Lock

problems.name: LFU Cache
problems.relevance: Same capacity-bounded shape as LRU but with a frequency-based selection policy — practice for swapping eviction strategies.
problems.description: get(key)/put(key,value) both O(1). On overflow, evict least-frequently-used; ties broken by least-recently-used.
hints: Two hash maps: key→(value,freq), freq→OrderedDict of keys at that freq
Track min_freq; on access, bump freq and move key between buckets
follow_ups: Compare to LRU — when does each policy win?
Add decay so old hits matter less

problems.name: Design Authentication Manager
problems.relevance: TTL-based registry — extends naturally to deregistering stale servers via heartbeat.
problems.description: generate(tokenId, currentTime), renew(tokenId, currentTime) (only if unexpired), countUnexpired(currentTime). TTL is fixed at construction.
constraints: At most 2000 calls, currentTime non-decreasing across calls
hints: Store tokenId → expiry. expiry = currentTime + timeToLive
countUnexpired can be O(n) here, or use an OrderedDict + lazy cleanup for amortized O(1)
follow_ups: Apply this pattern to deregister servers automatically when heartbeats expire
Make it thread-safe

problems.name: Design Underground System
problems.relevance: check-in / check-out tracking with running aggregation — same accumulator pattern as connection counts per server.
problems.description: checkIn(id, station, t), checkOut(id, station, t), getAverageTime(start, end). Tracks per-passenger trips and aggregates average duration per (start, end) pair.
hints: Two maps: id → (start, t_in), (start, end) → (sum, count)
On checkOut, look up the open trip, update aggregate, drop the open trip
follow_ups: Replace running sum/count with a sliding window over the last N minutes
Make thread-safe under concurrent check-in/check-out

problems.name: Encode and Decode TinyURL
problems.relevance: Direct URL Shortener — one of Revolut's three canonical live-coding rounds (Load Balancer, URL Shortener, Money Transfer).
problems.description: Design encode(longUrl) -> shortUrl and decode(shortUrl) -> longUrl such that decode(encode(x)) == x. Codes should be short and the system should handle collisions.
constraints: url is guaranteed to be a valid URL
hints: Two maps: long->short and short->long. Random 6-char base62 code.
Check for collisions before assigning. For idempotency, return the existing short URL if long URL already mapped.
follow_ups: Add MAX_URLS cap with LRU eviction
Make codes deterministic (hash of URL) instead of random
Add TTL per short URL with lazy cleanup
Make thread-safe with a Lock

problems.name: Design Hit Counter
problems.relevance: Sliding-window counter — the shape behind Revolut's withdrawal rate-limiter and 10-minute event-bucketing problems.
problems.description: hit(timestamp) records a hit at the given second. getHits(timestamp) returns hits in the past 5 minutes (300 seconds). Timestamps are monotonically non-decreasing.
constraints: Calls to hit and getHits are made in chronological order
hints: Deque of timestamps; on getHits, popleft while front <= timestamp - 300
For high-volume hits per second, bucket by second: 300-slot ring buffer of (timestamp, count)

complexity.time: Amortized O(1) per hit, O(k) per getHits where k = expired entries

follow_ups: Switch to a 300-slot ring buffer to bound memory regardless of hit rate
Generalize to arbitrary window size W
Adapt to a TokenBucketRateLimiter — same temporal-bucket idea
Make thread-safe under concurrent hit/getHits

problems.name: Logger Rate Limiter
problems.relevance: Per-key TTL gate — same shape as Revolut's per-user withdrawal rate limiter.
problems.description: shouldPrintMessage(timestamp, message) returns true iff this message has not been printed in the last 10 seconds. Timestamps are non-decreasing.
hints: Map message -> next-allowed-timestamp
To bound memory, evict expired entries lazily as timestamps advance
follow_ups: Bound memory: deque of (timestamp, message) with periodic eviction
Adapt to per-user request rate limiter (key by user_id)
Make thread-safe under concurrent calls

problems.name: Linked List Random Node
problems.relevance: Reservoir sampling — explicitly listed in Revolut's interview guide as 'select a random number from a stream with O(1) space'.
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list of unknown length, return a random node value. Each node must be selected with equal probability. Use O(1) space — you cannot store the full list.
hints: Reservoir sampling (k=1): for each node i (0-indexed), keep it with probability 1/(i+1)
Equivalent to random.randint(0, i) == 0 at each step
follow_ups: Generalize to reservoir sampling of size k
Apply to a streaming load balancer: pick a random server from an unknown-length stream of registrations
Weighted reservoir sampling (A-Res algorithm)

problems.name: All O`one Data Structure
problems.relevance: Frequency-bucket design — natural extension of LRU/LFU. Practice for swappable selection policies (max-key vs min-key) on the same backing store.
problems.description: Implement inc(key), dec(key), getMaxKey(), getMinKey() — all O(1). Keys start at count 0 (inc creates them). dec at count 1 removes the key.
hints: Doubly-linked list of count-buckets, each bucket holds a set of keys at that count
key->bucket map for O(1) lookup. Increment moves key to next bucket (insert if missing).
follow_ups: Adapt the bucket structure to LFU eviction — getMinKey is the eviction candidate
Add tie-breaking by insertion order (LRU-within-bucket)
Make thread-safe with a Lock

problems.name: Design A Leaderboard
problems.relevance: Score accumulator with top-K — same accumulator pattern as per-server connection counts; fintech leaderboard / top-account flavor.
problems.description: addScore(playerId, score) adds score to a player (creates if missing). top(K) returns sum of top K scores. reset(playerId) zeros the player's score.
hints: Hash map playerId -> score. top(K) = sum(heapq.nlargest(K, scores.values()))
For frequent top(K), maintain a SortedList keyed by score for O(K) reads

complexity.time: addScore/reset O(1), top O(n log K)

follow_ups: Make top(K) O(K log n) with a SortedList of (-score, playerId)
Add bottom(K) and rank(playerId)
Snapshot leaderboard at a given timestamp (versioned scores)
Make thread-safe under concurrent addScore/reset

problems.name: Design Circular Queue
problems.relevance: Fixed-capacity bounded buffer — fundamentals for capacity-bounded systems (URL Shortener cap, request buffer, ring-buffer rate limiters).
problems.description: Implement a circular queue of fixed size k with enQueue, deQueue, Front, Rear, isEmpty, isFull — all O(1).
hints: Array of size k + head index + size counter. Tail = (head + size) % k.
Avoid storing both head and tail — derive tail from size to avoid the empty-vs-full ambiguity.
follow_ups: Use as the backing store for the 300-slot ring-buffer Hit Counter
Add blocking variants (block on full/empty) using Condition variables
Make thread-safe with a Lock or use lock-free atomics

drill_plan.goal: Build muscle memory for the Revolut live-coding round (Load Balancer, URL Shortener, Money Transfer).

sessions.extension: Add max_size + CapacityExceededError. Now you have a single-strategy LoadBalancer.
Pull selection out into a Balancer Protocol. Implement RandomBalancer and RoundRobinBalancer.
Re-do as URL Shortener with MAX_URLS=100 — generate-and-check loop instead of integer pool.
Re-do as LeastConnectionsBalancer — heap keyed by active connection count.
Add TTL eviction. Compare to LFU policy.
Apply to LoadBalancer — auto-deregister servers whose heartbeat expired.
Add a single Lock. Then split into ReadWriteLock — reads (get) and writes (register/deregister).
Two-account transfer with consistent lock ordering. Test concurrent transfers in opposite directions.
